title: Artemis in Sierra
content:
آ آ آ آ Dramatis Personï؟½

آ آ آ آ Poet. Philosopher. Jones of Mariposa.



Poet
آ آ آ آ Halt! Here we are. Now wheel your mare a trifle
آ آ آ آ Just where you stand; then doff your hat and swear
آ آ آ آ Never yet was scene you might cover with your rifle
آ آ آ آ Half as complete or as marvelously fair.

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Dropped from Olympus or lifted out of Tempe,
آ آ آ آ Swung like a censer betwixt the earth and sky!
آ آ آ آ He who in Greece sang of flocks and flax and hemp, he
آ آ آ آ Here might recall them six thousand feet on high!

Poet
آ آ آ آ Well you may say so. The clamor of the river,
آ آ آ آ Hum of base toil, and manï؟½s ignoble strife,
آ آ آ آ Halt far below, where the stifling sunbeams quiver,
آ آ آ آ But never climb to this purer, higher life!

آ آ آ آ Not to this glade, where Jones of Mariposa,
آ آ آ آ Simple and meek as his flocks weï؟½re looking at,
آ آ آ آ Tends his soft charge; nor where his daughter Rosa
آ آ آ آ (A shot.)
آ آ آ آ Hallo! Whatï؟½s that?

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ A something throï؟½ my hat
آ آ آ آ Bullet, I think. You were speaking of his daughter?

Poet
آ آ آ آ Yes; but your hat you were moving through the leaves;
آ آ آ آ Likely he thought it some eagle bent on slaughter.
آ آ آ آ Lightly he shoots (A second shot.)

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ As one readily perceives.
آ آ آ آ Still, he improves! This time your hat has got it,
آ آ آ آ Quite near the band! Eh? Oh, just as you please
آ آ آ آ Stop, or go on.

Poet
آ آ آ آ Perhaps weï؟½d better trot it
آ آ آ آ Down through the hollow, and up among the trees.

Both
آ آ آ آ Trot, trot, trot, where the bullets cannot follow;
آ آ آ آ Trot down and up again among the laurel trees.

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Thanks, that is better; now of this shot-dispensing
آ آ آ آ Jones and his girl you were saying

Poet
آ آ آ آ Well, you see
آ آ آ آ I hang it all! Oh! whatï؟½s the use of fencing!
آ آ آ آ Sir, I confess it! these shots were meant for me.

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Are you mad!

Poet
آ آ آ آ God knows, I shouldnï؟½t wonder!
آ آ آ آ I love this coy nymph, who, coldly as yon peak
آ آ آ آ Shines on the river it feeds, yet keeps asunder
آ آ آ آ Long have I worshiped, but never dared to speak.

آ آ آ آ Till she, no doubt, her love no longer hiding,
آ آ آ آ Waked by some chance word her fatherï؟½s jealousy;
آ آ آ آ Slips her disdain as an avalanche down gliding
آ آ آ آ Sweeps flocks and kin away to clear a path for me.

آ آ آ آ Hence his attack.

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ I see. What I admire
آ آ آ آ Chiefly, I think, in your idyl, so to speak,
آ آ آ آ Is the cool modesty that checks your youthful fire,
آ آ آ آ Absence of self-love and abstinence of cheek!

آ آ آ آ Still, I might mention, Iï؟½ve met the gentle Rosa,
آ آ آ آ Danced with her thrice, to her fatherï؟½s jealous dread;
آ آ آ آ And, it is possible, sheï؟½s happened to disclose a
آ آ آ آ Ahem! You can fancy why he shoots at me instead.

Poet
آ آ آ آ You?

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Me. But kindly take your hand from your revolver,
آ آ آ آ I am not choleric but accidents may chance.
آ آ آ آ And hereï؟½s the father, who alone can be the solver
آ آ آ آ Of this twin riddle of the hat and the romance.

آ آ آ آ [Enter Jones of Mariposa.]

Poet
آ آ آ آ Speak, shepherd mine!

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Hail! Time-and-cartridge waster,
آ آ آ آ Aimless exploder of theories and skill!
آ آ آ آ Whom do you shoot?

Jones of Mariposa
آ آ آ آ Well, shootinï؟½ ainï؟½t my taste, or
آ آ آ آ Ef I shoot anything I only shoot to kill.

آ آ آ آ That ainï؟½t whatï؟½s up. I only kem to tell ye
آ آ آ آ Sportinï؟½ or courtinï؟½ trot homeward for your life!
آ آ آ آ Gals will be gals, and pï؟½rï؟½aps itï؟½s just ez well ye
آ آ آ آ Larned there was one had no wish to be a wife.

Poet
آ آ آ آ What?

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Is this true?

Jones of Mariposa
آ آ آ آ I reckon it looks like it.
آ آ آ آ She saw ye cominï؟½. My gun was standinï؟½ by;
آ آ آ آ She made a grab, and ï؟½fore I up could strike it,
آ آ آ آ Blazed at ye both! The critter is so shy!

Poet
آ آ آ آ Who?

Jones of Mariposa
آ آ آ آ My darter!

Philosopher
آ آ آ آ Rosa?

Jones of Mariposa
آ آ آ آ  Same! Good-by!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Bret Harte (Francis) delivers a powerful performance in "Artemis in Sierra"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
